hypothesis: An adaptive proportional–integral controller that updates similarity-, fragment-, and n-gram penalty thresholds at each RL step to maintain setpoints of mean Tanimoto similarity ≤0.14 and unique-fragment ratio ≥0.95 of their initial values will produce a final average singlet‑fission score within 2% of the baseline (no-constraint) REINVENT4 run after 1000 steps while preventing diversity collapse across all NaviDiv metrics.
